subject: [Pre-read] Reg CB LFF/ CNLS/ Projects Monthly Update - Aug 2025
deck_filename: Reg CB LFF_ CNLS_ Projects Monthly Catchup - Aug 2025_vf.pdf

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Please find attached slides for our discussion tomorrow, let me know for
any questions.

Thank you.

-- 
*Jason YU*
*Regional CB | MY & SG Cross Border*
*Mobile*: +65 90284825
*Email*: jason.yu@shopee.com

Hi Ian, All,

Please see follow ups from our discussions yesterday, let me know if
anything.

No. Topic Action Items PIC DDL
1 TH TISI Impact Follow up with TH on necessity to impose such strict
restrictions on FBS WH to avoid potential back-and-forth in the future;
Share VN example Jason 22 August
2 CB FBS Inbound Capacity Benchmark space over ado metric across markets
and work with Reg WH and local WH team to increase capacity as needed;
Follow up with Jiahao on TH capacity increase plan Jason 29 August
3 CB FBS Free Trial Align with Mohit's team on free trial mechanics to
secure long-term seller commitment to FBS and propose plan for CB across
markets Jason 15 Sep
4 CNLS Seller Movement Structurally reassess site expansion opportunities
and prioritizations, and to optimize organization of programs and action
plans Ray 5 September
5 CNLS FBS Improve local fbs onboarding process benchmarking against cb fbs
Ray 5 September
6 Counter Taobao Program - Validate accuracy of current price comp data
- Validate assortment match rate data with further sample checks
- Continue to push assortment gap closure and price competitiveness while
exploring more dimensions to close TB gaps Jason 29 August

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 65

   - Our match rate seem to have increase by quite a bit over time (17%
   from Apr >> July), do we know what caused this?

Were we really able to
   source more SKUs?
Or were we just better over matchin?

Slide 69

   - In our NPC, do we compare TB vs entire Shopee?
Or against CB?

- Do we have a sense why price competitiveness is so bad for Furniture
   and F&B in particular?
- After we reduce SLS mark up to 5% profits, how would the NPC look?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Slide 65

Our match rate seem to have increase by quite a bit over time (17% from Apr
SKUs? Or were we just better over matchin?

The increase in match rate was driven by improved sourcing efforts. Between
Apr and Jul, we sourced approximately 37k SKUs directly through sellers,
contributing an estimated +9% uplift in match rate. The remaining of the
match rate growth was still driven organically by sellers' own listing. The
matching methodology remains consistent.


Slide 69

In our NPC, do we compare TB vs entire Shopee? Or against CB?

We are currently comparing TB against all CB sellers, since our push
efforts are targeted at CB RMs/sellers. We are also adding another view to
include local sellers to have a complete view of Shopee vs TB NPC.

Do we have a sense why price competitiveness is so bad for Furniture and
F&B in particular?

Not quite sure what is causing the NPC issue for F&B, especially in SG and
MY.

Overall, for Furniture & F&B, our price competitiveness is lower mainly
because:

   -

   Sellers’ COGS and seller margins on SHP are ~1.2x-1.5x higher compared
   to TB (50% higher than Fashion/Beauty)
   -

   Logistics fees: SHP’s hidden fees plus BPSF add up to be 17% higher than
   TB. (Bigger impact than Fashion/Beauty)
   -

   Commission rates: SHP charges a commission rate of 14-20%, while TB’s
   rate is just 2% for F&B and 5% for Furniture

After we reduce SLS mark up to 5% profits, how would the NPC look? (Am
assuming this is the main issue for furniture right now).

Assuming the SLS markup is reduced to 5% profit margin, NPC is expected to
improve to 1%. (+8% from existing NPC at -7%).

content.question:
Do we have a sense why price competitiveness is so bad for Furniture and
F&B in particular?

Not quite sure what is causing the NPC issue for F&B, especially in SG and
MY.

Overall, for Furniture & F&B, our price competitiveness is lower mainly
because:

   - Sellers’ COGS and seller margins on SHP are ~1.2x-1.5x higher compared
   to TB (50% higher than Fashion/Beauty)

Is this a function of commission rate being higher?

Or just sellers want to
earn more?

- Logistics fees: SHP’s hidden fees plus BPSF add up to be 17% higher
   than TB. (Bigger impact than Fashion/Beauty)
   - Commission rates: SHP charges a commission rate of 14-20%, while TB’s
   rate is just 2% for F&B and 5% for Furniture



Is this something we want to relook at?
To be more competitive?

Esp for
Furniture?
After we reduce SLS mark up to 5% profits, how would the NPC look?

(Am
assuming this is the main issue for furniture right now).

Assuming the SLS markup is reduced to 5% profit margin, NPC is expected to
improve to 1%. (+8% from existing NPC at -7%).



Is this for furniture or overall NPC?
